meaning: n.演说者,演讲者,雄辩家,[律]原告,请愿人
examples: He was a natural politician, a gifted orator who knew how to work a crowd.
He also shone as an orator in Parliament.
"When Mary found this garden it looked quite dead," the orator proceeded.
He wrote beautifully and, after overcoming a stammer, was a wonderful orator: humorous, self-deprecating, empathetic.
He remains a great orator, with a powerful sense, if questionable grasp, of history.
